entry.title: [AP-SOUTHEAST-SYD2][Public Cloud] - Planned decommission of the region
entry.content:
THIS IS A SCHEDULED EVENT Aug 15, 06:00 - 16:00 UTC

May 28, 09:48 UTC
Scheduled - Start time : 15/08/2026 06:00 UTC
End time : 15/08/2026 16:00 UTC
Service impact : Public Cloud instances hosted in the AP-SOUTHEAST-SYD-2 region
Service improvement : As part of the continuous evolution of our Public Cloud offering, OVHcloud will progressively phase out the AP-SOUTHEAST-SYD-2 region in Sydney and focus future developments on the SYD1 region.
Services hosted in AP-SOUTHEAST-SYD-2 will enter maintenance mode before a definitive shutdown in the coming months.
Affected customers must manually migrate their workloads and data to the SYD1 region to avoid service disruption.

Required customer actions
Customers are required to:
- Create new instances in the SYD1 region
- Manually migrate applications, services, and data
- Validate workloads on the new infrastructure
- Stop and delete old instances once migration is completed

We strongly recommend performing backups before starting the migration process.
Migration documentation:
https://help.ovhcloud.com/csm/en-ie-public-cloud-compute-migration-between-az?id=kb_article_view&sysparm_article=KB0073126

To compensate for the inconvenience, OVHcloud will automatically apply a credit corresponding to one month of the affected services usage.

entry.title: [GLOBAL][Containers & Orchestration] - End of Life of Load Balancer for Managed Kubernetes Service notification
entry.content:
THIS IS A SCHEDULED EVENT Jul 9, 08:00 - 16:00 UTC

Jun 4, 16:27 UTC
Scheduled - As part of the end-of-life of our Containers & Orchestration - Load Balancer (IOLB) offer, a maintenance is scheduled in BHS5 / DE1 / GRA5 / GRA7 / GRA9 / GRA11 / SBG5 / SGP1 / SYD1 / WAW1 / UK1 / US-EAST-VA / US-WEST-OR regions.

Start time : 09/07/2026 08:00 UTC
End time : 09/07/2026 16:00 UTC
Service impact : We're retiring the historical Load Balancer for Managed Kubernetes, now replaced by the Public Cloud Loadbalancer. As part of this change, resources will be deleted and no longer billed.
Note: the migration to Public Cloud Loadbalancer is not automatic and must be performed on your side. (https://docs.ovhcloud.com/en/guides/public-cloud/containers-orchestration/managed-kubernetes/migrate-iolb-to-public-cloud-loadbalancer)
Service improvement : As part of our continuous improvement policy, we will be doing a maintenance on our Containers & Orchestration - Load Balancer in the specified regions.

Thank you for your understanding.

entry.title: [RBX/SBG][Containers & Orchestration] - Managed Rancher Service maintenance notification
entry.content:
THIS IS A SCHEDULED EVENT Jul 1, 07:30 - 16:00 UTC

Apr 22, 17:38 UTC
Scheduled - As part of our continuous improvement plan, maintenance is scheduled on our Containers & Orchestration offer.

Start time : 01/07/2026 07:30 UTC
End time : 01/07/2026 16:00 UTC
Service impact : None
Service improvement : As part of our continuous improvement policy, we need to upgrade Ranchers in version 2.11 to version 2.12. You can already manage the upgrade by yourself through OVHcloud Control panel or API.
The Rancher compatibility matrix with downstream clusters will change, indeed K8S version 1.30 will no longer be officially supported.
For more information regarding Managed Rancher Service supported versions and lifecycle policy, you can visit this page: KB0064608

Thank you for your understanding.

entry.title: [GLOBAL][Data & Analytics] - Data Platform maintenance notification
entry.content:
THIS IS A SCHEDULED EVENT Jun 15, 12:00 - 15:00 UTC

Jun 3, 17:20 UTC
Scheduled - As part of our continuous improvement plan, a maintenance is scheduled on our Data Platform offer.

Here are the details of the maintenance:
Start time : 15/06/2026 12:00 UTC
End time : 15/06/2026 15:00 UTC
Service impact : None
Service improvement : As part of our continuous improvement policy, we will be doing a maintenance on our Data Platform infrastructure.

Thank you for your understanding.

entry.title: [EU-WEST-PAR][Public Cloud] - Network connectivity issues notification
entry.content:
Jun 10, 18:01 UTC
Resolved - We are pleased to inform you that the incident affecting our Dedicated Servers offering has been resolved.

Start time : 09/06/2026 07:00 UTC
End time : 10/06/2026 17:55 UTC
Root Cause : A service disruption occurred due to an unexpected underlying infrastructure malfunction.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

Jun 10, 15:20 UTC
Identified - We are currently experiencing an ongoing incident. We have determined the origin of the issue affecting our Public Cloud offer on the specific EU-WEST-PAR region.

Here are some supplementary details :

Start time : 09/06/2026 07:00 UTC
Impacted Service(s) : Nodes located in EU-WEST-PAR region may experience packets drop while communicating with their controlplanes and the Internet.
Customers Impact : Customer are temporarily facing connectivity issues with their nodes located in the specified region.
Root Cause : A service disruption occurred due to an unexpected underlying infrastructure malfunction.
Ongoing Actions : The incident has been identified and our teams are mobilised to restore service as quickly as possible.

We will keep you updated on the progress and resolution.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

entry.title: [EU/CA][Compute - Instance] - Instances operating system maintenance notification
entry.content:
Jun 10, 14:49 UTC
Completed - All services are operational. No more impact since 10/06/2026 14:47 UTC.

Jun 10, 14:00 UTC
In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

Jun 5, 12:41 UTC
Update - As part of our continuous improvement plan, a maintenance is scheduled on our Compute - Instance offer for the EU and CA regions.

Start time : 10/06/2026 14:00 UTC
End time : 10/06/2026 16:00 UTC
Service impact : The operating systems Ubuntu 25.04 and Fedora 42 are end-of-life.
Customers who have already installed one of these operating systems may continue to use it, but will no longer be able to reinstall it.
Service improvement : As part of our continuous improvement policy, starting from June 1st, customers will no longer be able to install end-of-life operating systems.
We encourage customers using these operating systems to migrate to supported versions such as Ubuntu 26.04 or Fedora 44, or a later version.

You can find the information at the following links:
https://github.com/ovh/public-cloud-roadmap/issues/1144
https://github.com/ovh/public-cloud-roadmap/issues/1158

Thank you for your understanding.

May 28, 12:04 UTC
Scheduled - As part of our continuous improvement plan, a maintenance is scheduled on our Compute - Instance offer for the EU and CA regions.

Start time : 10/06/2026 14:00 UTC
End time : 10/06/2026 16:00 UTC
Service impact : The operating systems Ubuntu 25.04 and Fedora 42 are end-of-life.
Customers who have already installed one of these operating systems may continue to use it, but will no longer be able to reinstall it.
Service improvement : As part of our continuous improvement policy, starting from June 1st, customers will no longer be able to install end-of-life operating systems.
We encourage customers using these operating systems to migrate to supported versions such as Ubuntu 26.04 or Fedora 44, or a later version.

You can find the information at the following links:
https://github.com/ovh/public-cloud-roadmap/issues/1144
https://github.com/ovh/public-cloud-roadmap/issues/1158

Thank you for your understanding.

entry.title: [SBG5][Storage] - Block storage incident notification
entry.content:
Jun 10, 08:26 UTC
Resolved - We are pleased to inform you that the incident affecting our Block storage offering has been resolved.

Start time : 10/06/2026 00:50 UTC
End time : 10/06/2026 07:10 UTC
Root Cause : A service disruption occurred due to an unexpected underlying infrastructure malfunction.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

Jun 10, 01:30 UTC
Monitoring - Service has been fully restored since 10/06/2026 01:26 UTC. Monitoring remains active to ensure long-term stability.

Here are some supplementary details :

Start time : 10/06/2026 00:50 UTC
Impacted Service(s) : Block Storage service experienced degraded performance.
Customers Impact : Customers may have experienced increased latency, slower response times, or intermittent errors when accessing or managing data stored on the affected Block Storage.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

Jun 10, 01:25 UTC
Identified - We are currently experiencing an ongoing incident. We have determined the origin of the issue affecting our Block storage offer in SBG5.

Please find the details of this incident below :
Start time : 10/06/2026 00:50 UTC
Impacted Service(s) : Block storage service is experiencing degraded performance.
Customers Impact : Customers may experience increased latency, slower response times, or intermittent errors when accessing or managing data stored on the affected Block Storage.
Root Cause : A service disruption occurred due to an unexpected underlying infrastructure malfunction.
Ongoing Actions : The incident has been identified and our teams are mobilised to restore service as quickly as possible.

We will keep you updated on the progress and resolution.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

entry.title: [Global][PCI-Databases] - MongoDB incident notification
entry.content:
Jun 5, 06:26 UTC
Resolved - We are pleased to inform you that the incident affecting our PCI-Databases offering has been resolved.

Start time : 03/06/2026 12:00 UTC
End time : 04/06/2026 12:00 UTC
Root Cause : A service disruption occurred due to an unexpected underlying infrastructure malfunction.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

Jun 4, 12:07 UTC
Monitoring - Service has been fully restored since 04/06/2026 12:00 UTC. Monitoring remains active to ensure long-term stability.

Here are some supplementary details :

Start time : 03/06/2026 12:00 UTC
Impacted Service(s) : Some MongoDB databases that initially ran with 3 nodes temporarily ran with 2 nodes
Customers Impact : Customers with 3-node MongoDB services temporarily operated with only 2 nodes. No latency or data loss was observed
Root Cause : A service disruption occurred due to an unexpected underlying infrastructure malfunction.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

Jun 4, 09:33 UTC
Identified - We are currently experiencing an ongoing incident. We have determined the origin of the issue affecting our PCI-Databases offer on the specific global region.

Here are some supplementary details :

Start time : 03/06/2026 12:00 UTC
Impacted Service(s) : Some MongoDB databases that initially run with 3 nodes may temporarily run with 2 nodes.
Customers Impact : Customers with 3 node MongoDB services may temporarily operate with only 2 nodes. No latency or data loss is observed.
Root Cause : A service disruption occurred due to an unexpected underlying infrastructure malfunction.
Ongoing Actions : The incident has been identified and our teams are mobilised to restore service as quickly as possible.

We will keep you updated on the progress and resolution.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

Jun 4, 09:28 UTC
Investigating - We are currently investigating an incident affecting our PCI-Databases offering, which is causing temporary degraded issue in the Global region.

Here are some supplementary details :

Start time : 03/06/2026 12:00 UTC
Impacted Service(s) : Some MongoDB databases that initially run with 3 nodes may temporarily run with 2 nodes.
Customers Impact : Customers with 3 node MongoDB services may temporarily operate with only 2 nodes. No latency or data loss is observed.
Ongoing Actions : Our teams are investigating to determine the origin of the incident and fix it.

We will keep you updated on the progress and resolution.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

entry.title: [GLOBAL][Identity, Security & Operations] - Security patches deployed on OpenStack Identity service (Keystone)
entry.content:
May 29, 07:25 UTC
Update - Scheduled maintenance is still in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

May 28, 15:02 UTC
In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

May 28, 15:01 UTC
Scheduled - As part of our ongoing security maintenance operations, emergency patches were deployed approximately 48 hours ago on the OpenStack Identity service (Keystone) to address five recently disclosed CVEs.

These vulnerabilities were publicly disclosed today at 15:00 UTC.

The applied upstream patches are known to introduce behavioral changes and regressions, particularly impacting:
- Trusts
- Application Credentials

Customers using these features are advised to verify that their applications, automations, and authentication workflows continue to operate as expected.

If you experience issues related to these changes, please contact our support team and provide details about your workflow so we can assist you in identifying compatible alternatives or adjustments.

Please note that these regressions are intentional upstream security changes required to mitigate the vulnerabilities. According to the upstream project maintainers, reverting or modifying this behavior is currently not planned, as it could reintroduce the security risks.

We continue to monitor the situation closely.

entry.title: [AP-SOUTH-MUM/EU-SOUTH-MIL/EU-WEST-PAR/RBX][Containers & Orchestration] - Managed Kubernetes Service Incident Notification
entry.content:
May 28, 14:25 UTC
Resolved - We are pleased to inform you that the incident affecting our Containers & Orchestration - Managed Kubernetes Service offering in AP-SOUTH-MUM / EU-SOUTH-MIL / EU-WEST-PAR / RBX regions has been resolved.

Start time : 27/05/2026 14:00 UTC
End time : 28/05/2026 13:56 UTC

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

May 27, 16:02 UTC
Identified - We are currently experiencing an ongoing incident. We have determined the origin of the issue affecting our Containers & Orchestration - Managed Kubernetes Service in AP-SOUTH-MUM / EU-SOUTH-MIL / EU-WEST-PAR / RBX regions.

Here are some supplementary details :

Start time : 27/05/2026 14:00 UTC
Impacted Service(s) : MKS cluster delivery and deletion is currently unavailable.
Customers Impact : Customers are currently unable to create or delete new MKS clusters.
Root Cause : A service disruption is currently occurring due to an unexpected infrastructure malfunction.
Ongoing Actions : The incident has been identified and our teams are mobilised to restore service as quickly as possible.

We will keep you updated on the progress and resolution.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

May 27, 15:27 UTC
Investigating - We are currently experiencing an event affecting our Managed Kubernetes Service in AP-SOUTH-MUM / EU-SOUTH-MIL / EU-WEST-PAR / RBX regions.

Start time : 27/05/2026 14:00 UTC

Our teams are fully committed to investigating this issue and working towards a resolution as soon as possible. As investigations are ongoing, we will share any new findings or updates with you as soon as possible.

We apologize for any inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding.

entry.title: [EU-WEST-PAR-A /RBX-A/GRA/SBG5/US-WEST-OR][Storage] - Block storage maintenance notification
entry.content:
May 27, 18:02 UTC
Completed - The scheduled maintenance has been completed.

May 25, 09:00 UTC
In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

May 21, 20:37 UTC
Scheduled - As part of our continuous improvement plan, maintenance is scheduled on our Block Storage offer in regions listed below :

EU-WEST-PAR-A
GRA11
GRA3
GRA5
GRA7
GRA9
RBX-A
SBG5
US-WEST-OR

Start time : 25/05/2026 09:00 UTC
End time : 28/05/2026 17:00 UTC
Service impact : During the maintenance window, customers may experience a temporary performance degradation.
Service improvement : As part of our continuous improvement policy and to improve service quality, we will be doing a software upgrade on our Block Storage servers.

Thank you for your understanding.

entry.title: [BHS1/3][Compute - Instance] - Some instances incident notification
entry.content:
May 27, 17:57 UTC
Resolved - We had an incident on our Compute - Instance offer in BHS1 and BHS3 regions which has now been resolved.

Here are some supplementary details :

Start time : 27/05/2026 15:46 UTC
End time : 27/05/2026 17:29 UTC
Impacted Service(s) : Some instances in BHS1 and BHS3 regions were unreachable.
Customers Impact : Some customers were temporarily unable to access and use their instances in the specified regions.
Root Cause : A service disruption occurred due to an unexpected underlying infrastructure malfunction.

We thank you for your understanding and patience throughout this incident.
